items.summary: Iran's sports minister says his country cannot compete in the men's FIFA World Cup, which is co-hosted by the United States.

items.title: Why IV drips are surging in popularity in Australia's wellness space
items.summary: Some advocates say vitamin IV drips can promote clearer thinking, immunity, beauty and even anti-aging, but health experts say there is little research to back the claims.

items.title: More than 100 doctors file no-confidence vote in regional health service executive management
items.summary: Two top doctors and a nurse have left Albury Wodonga hospital in just six months, prompting a vote of no confidence in management.

items.title: North Queensland residents say electoral boundary changes leave them 'short-changed'
items.summary: With one parliamentarian from the far north set to go, residents in some areas will go from being 15 minutes' drive away from their local MP to 15 hours.

items.title: Bowen says Middle East conflict a 'crisis' for fuel supply — as it happened
items.summary: Energy Minister Chris Bowen has told Question Time that the Middle East conflict is presenting a fuel supply "crisis".

items.title: Victorian Farmers Federation president Brett Hosking announces shock exit
items.summary: Victorian Farmers Federation president Brett Hosking resigns amid rumours he is seeking political pre-selection.

items.title: Chinchilla residents told not to return to flood-affected areas as Bundaberg counts cost
items.summary: Residents in Chinchilla are being told not to return to some areas as major flooding continues.

items.title: Sydney's abnormal heat, humidity levels due to warm Tasman Sea and northerly winds
items.summary: Sydney's humidity has been rivalling the tropics in the middle of wet season, with the stifling conditions expected to linger into autumn.

items.title: ABC Oscars quiz: How well do you remember the winners and losers from the past 10 years?
items.summary: Test your knowledge of who went home with a statue and who was honoured just to be nominated.

items.title: Adelaide Hills residents living near fertiliser firm Neutrog want answers on air quality
items.summary: Residents in the Adelaide Hills town of Kanmantoo and surrounds have been dealing with a growing regularity of pungent odour but the fertiliser company, who pleaded guilty to environmental breaches, says its chicken litter presents no risk.

items.title: Kalyn Ponga makes international rugby league switch to represent New Zealand
items.summary: Kalyn Ponga says previous appearances for Australia did not reflect his true national identity after the International Rugby League approved his application to switch allegiances to New Zealand.

items.title: Iconic Australian-made pinball machines preserved for generations to come
items.summary: Five pinball machines, including the first licensed Star Wars unit, have been saved from the scrap heap and will eventually be made available for the public to play at a special exhibition.

items.title: Kharg Island could be Iran's weak spot as Washington reportedly considers ground invasion options
items.summary: There is a small island in the Persian Gulf that is vital to the flow of Iranian oil to its customers and, analysts say, if Washington or Israel were to seize it the regime's war chest would soon dry up.

items.title: Record 400 million barrels of oil to be released from reserves to stall price hike
items.summary: The International Energy Agency agrees to release 400 million barrels of oil, the largest such move in its history. Australia says it is considering the decision, which would not involve sending oil overseas.

items.title: Approval granted for major expansion of Porters Bridge Road Quarry despite concerns
items.summary: Locals say the "continuous rumbling" from a quarry in Tasmania's Meander Valley is a major disturbance. The company which owns it has just been given approval to increase production by 500 per cent.

items.title: Victorian wineries sell vineyards to gold exploration company amid global headwinds
items.summary: As the wine industry struggles with increased challenges, some Victorian wineries are choosing to sell their land to mining companies.

items.title: The real-life story behind The Voice of Hind Rajab
items.summary: Protests erupted following the killing of six-year-old Hind Rajab at the hands of Israeli forces. Two years later, her story is in the running for an Academy Award.

items.title: Victorian government vows to shift building inspection costs from buyers to sellers
items.summary: The Victorian government says it will make home sellers pay for building and pest inspections rather than home buyers if it wins this year's state election in November.

items.title: Matt Canavan may blunt One Nation but can he help rebuild the Coalition?
items.summary: Matt Canavan's rise to the Nationals leadership gives the Coalition a fighter capable of taking on Pauline Hanson, but beating back One Nation is only part of the opposition's electoral dilemma.

items.title: Former spy boss Dennis Richardson resigns from antisemitism royal commission
items.summary: Dennis Richardson was originally tapped by Prime Minister Anthony Albanese in the weeks after the Bondi terror attack to lead a review into the federal government's intelligence and security agencies.

items.title: Bird deaths linked to fish lost in algal bloom, SA Research and Development Institute says
items.summary: Birds are starving and dying along metropolitan Adelaide beaches, caused by a lack of fish as a result of the algal bloom, the South Australian government says.

items.title: World’s oil supply threatened
items.summary: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

items.title: Tasmania Police officer and a Franklin River blockade photographer reunite after four decades
items.summary: Ian Wright captured the Franklin River blockade on film. Forty years later, the officer who arrested him reached out.

items.title: Wednesday finance with Alan Kohler
items.summary: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

items.title: Flower importers feel the pinch as Iran war affects flight routes
items.summary: The war in the Middle East is not just affecting petrol prices and travel plans — florists and their suppliers have suddenly found themselves unable to supply blooms due to the disruption of air freight routes.

items.title: More views of SA Liberal candidate emerge saying Islam 'poisonous, wrong', 'trans agenda is insanity'
items.summary: More views by Liberal Party candidate and evangelical Christian Carston Woodhouse emerge with the Adelaide man saying Islam is "poisonous and wrong" and the "trans agenda is insanity". Warning: This story contains content some readers might find distressing.

items.title: Mother of Iranian women's footballer pleads with player to stay in Australia as families face threats
items.summary: The smiles and joy seen in pictures of the seven members of the Iranian women's football team who sought asylum in Australia only tell part of the story, with many still scared for their families back home.

items.title: Economists scramble to change their interest rate forecasts after RBA speaks
items.summary: Expectations the Reserve Bank will hike the cash rate next week are rising as leading economists shift their forecasts — and some say one increase may not be enough.

items.title: The Tasmania Devils won't play AFL at Macquarie Point earlier than 2031. Should we be surprised?
items.summary: Tasmania's future AFL club believed they would be playing in their new $1.13-billion stadium from 2030, but it's now clear their debut there will be at least one year later.

items.title: Women tell court Barry Cable sexually assaulted them as children in Melbourne backyard spas
items.summary: Former footballer Barry Cable sexually assaulted children in a backyard spa in Melbourne at a time when he was a "famous" figure, a court has heard.

items.title: Aerial footage of Darwin River floods
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Assessing the flood damage at Darwin River
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Matt Canavan is National Party's unusual leader with dreams of fossil-fuel powered future
items.summary: Matt Canavan once claimed to be an atypical Nationals member. Now the pro-mining Queenslander is leading the party from the Senate.

items.title: Nicholas James Alexander jailed over antisemitic attacks across Sydney
items.summary: Nicholas James Alexander was jailed for a maximum of five years for his role orchestrating a series of antisemitic attacks across Sydney, including the fire-bombing of a childcare centre.

items.title: Boil water alert remains for Darwin area after flood takes out dam pump
items.summary: People in Darwin, Palmerstorn and its surrounding areas will need to keep boiling water before consumption for the foreseeable future, as work to restore the Darwin River Dam's pumps continue.

items.title: Doongmabulla Springs case proceeds to civil trial in Queensland's Supreme Court
items.summary: The dispute centres on the Doongmbulla Springs, near the Carmichael River in central Queensland, with traditional custodians accusing the Queensland government of failing to protect the sacred site.

items.title: Sarah Game tells voters not to put her party first after split with Chris McDermott
items.summary: A South Australian politician is urging voters to not back her own party in the upper house after a dramatic breakdown with star candidate and former Adelaide Crows captain Chris McDermott.

items.title: Government doubles graduate visa fee but Pacific Island countries exempt
items.summary: Tiered pricing for visas is being used to support Pacific nations but some fear it could be misused to target certain communities.

items.title: Linq Buslines and two directors plead guilty to safety breaches after deadly crash
items.summary: The bus company linked to the deadly 2023 Hunter Valley wedding bus crash and a director plead guilty to heavy-vehicle law safety breaches.

items.title: Adelaide man Craig Sansbury-Weetra avoids jail for 'cowardly act' during CBD attack
items.summary: An Adelaide man will serve a home detention sentence for an attack in the city's nightlife precinct that left his "off-guard" victim learning how to walk and eat independently again.

items.title: Fuel shortages hit rural Queensland businesses
items.summary: Growing fuel uncertainty is hitting rural and fisheries businesses across regional Queensland, potentially costing them thousands in lost production and higher operating costs.

items.title: Perth Festival 2026: Our favourites from Haribo Kimchi to Ngaiire
items.summary: Operatic punk, a Korean snack stand on stage, and a return to the golden age of dance halls were some of the highlights of the festival in 2026.

items.title: Man accused of murdering mother in Gunnedah caravan park to face Supreme Court trial
items.summary: Callan Davidson, charged with domestic violence-related murder after the body of his mother Cheryl Davidson was found at a caravan park, has pleaded not guilty before local court today.

items.title: Darwin River residents say they have 'lost everything' in flooding that struck without warning
items.summary: People have had to be rescued from croc-inhabited waters and some have lost everything following flooding in Darwin River.

items.title: Cody Schaeffer, former youth advocate, pleads guilty to assaulting five teenagers
items.summary: The court heard Cody Schaeffer, who founded Borderline Australia, paid each boy to be "hogtied" and "have their feet slapped with coat hangers".

items.title: Anonymous gamblers betting on Iran strikes make millions on Polymarket
items.summary: Gambling experts and US lawmakers have raised concerns about the ethics of online betting and whether insider information is helping profiteers cash in on war.

items.title: WOMADelaide 2026 review: How a city found joy in a global music celebration
items.summary: At Adelaide's global music weekender, tens of thousands transform everyday anxieties into a shared burst of joy.

items.title: Farmers devastated by NT floods feel overlooked by emergency response
items.summary: NT farmers counting the cost of livestock deaths and crop damage say they feel overlooked by the emergency flood response.

items.title: Tony Popovic turns attention to finer details as Socceroos close in on 2026 FIFA World Cup
items.summary: Socceroos coach Tony Popovic is known for his attention to detail and is leaving no stone unturned as he prepares his squad for the World Cup.

items.title: Adelaide criminal defence lawyer Andrew Graham vows to contest rape charge
items.summary: Adelaide criminal defence lawyer Andrew Robert Graham says he will fight a charge of rape, saying the allegation "is untrue".

items.title: Australian Federal Police officer pleads guilty to making false sworn legal statements before trial
items.summary: Detective Leading Senior Constable Trent Madders was due to face trial next week, accused of perjury, concealing evidence and perverting the course of justice, related to incidents in June 2019 and August 2020.

items.title: Doctor expresses concern for memory of driver who crashed into group of people in Daylesford
items.summary: The diabetes doctor of the driver responsible for a 2023 collision in the Victorian town of Daylesford, that killed five people, tells a coronial inquest that he is "concerned" for his patient's memory.

items.title: Australia's pesticide regulator sets new rules banning some rat baits from public sale
items.summary: Rat baits could soon be restricted to licensed operators after a change in regulations from the Australian Pesticides and Veterinary Medicines Authority.

items.title: Brisbane Roar offers training and support to Iranian asylum seeker players
items.summary: A-League clubs reach out to the Iranian women's players granted asylum, offering training, support and a new "home".

items.title: St Basil's aged care home in Fawkner fined $150k over breaches relating to fatal COVID outbreak
items.summary: St Basil's aged care in Fawkner has been fined $150,000 for failing to provide adequate training to staff in 2020.

items.title: Matt Canavan vows to 'fight back' as he rises to the Nationals leadership
items.summary: With a populist economic message, the new Nationals leader said local manufacturing and higher birth rates were key as he rejected the labels of "left" and "right" politics.

items.title: Indian Wells 2026: Talia Gibson scores upset win over Jasmine Paolini, Aryna Sabalenka outduels Naomi Osaka
items.summary: Talia Gibson scores one of the biggest wins of her young career as she advances at Indian Wells, while Aryna Sabalenka shows off one of the newest members of her team.

items.title: SA Liberal Party candidate says 'same-sex marriage is not real' and 'feminism is demonic'
items.summary: A Liberal candidate says "same-sex marriage is not real" and "feminism is demonic", in views Liberal leader Ashton Hurn says she does not share, but will leave up to voters to decide. Warning: This story contains content some readers might find distressing.

items.title: Major floods threaten NT remote communities of Daly River and Beswick, as return to school begins in Katherine
items.summary: Schools around Katherine are starting to reopen in the wake of flooding over the weekend, though major flooding is continuing to threaten a remote Northern Territory community nearby.

items.title: There is a constant reminder in Roebourne of a massacre 160 years ago
items.summary: In the Pilbara town of Roebourne the name of many streets haunt residents as they are named after perpetrators of a massacre which decimated the Yaburara tribe 160 years ago.

items.title: Indigenous Dance College NAISDA celebrates 50 years of cultural pride
items.summary: Australia's longest-running First Nations dance academy has helped to shape the cultural fabric of the nation.

items.title: NACC Robodebt investigation finds two people engaged in serious corrupt conduct
items.summary: Two people investigated over the so-called "Robodebt" scheme have been found to have engaged in serious corrupt conduct, while four others, including former prime minister Scott Morrison, have been cleared.

items.title: Australian designer Katie Perry wins trademark case against popstar Katy Perry in High Court
items.summary: Australian fashion designer Katie Perry wins her High Court battle against international pop star Katy Perry in a long-running trademark case.

items.title: Member of Iranian football team granted asylum requests return to Iran
items.summary: Home Affairs Minister Tony Burke says one of the seven members of the Iranian women's football delegation granted asylum in Australia has changed their mind and wants to return to Iran. Iranian embassy officials have collected the woman from the hotel where she was under police protection.

items.title: UEFA Champions League: Spurs keeper hooked after 17 minutes against Atletico, Liverpool upset by Galatasaray, Yamal penalty denies Newcastle
items.summary: Tottenham's nightmare season drops to an embarrassing new low as Antonin Kinsky is marched off the field by his manager in Madrid.

items.title: Family members of people killed in e-rideable accidents left disappointed by lack of urgency from authorities
items.summary: The families of two West Australians killed in e-scooter accidents say they are disappointed the state government is not acting sooner to better regulate e-rideable devices.

items.title: Australian Medical Association speaks out after top doctor suspended from Albury Wodonga Health
items.summary: The Australian Medical Association is reviewing why several top doctors and nurses have left Albury Wodonga hospital.

items.title: Women with Asherman's syndrome call for tighter control of outdated practices
items.summary: Outdated medical devices are being used to perform a common obstetrics procedure on Australian women that can cause a condition known as Asherman's syndrome.
